# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:latest

# Install necessary packages
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    curl \
    bash \
    docker-cli

# Create a non-root user
RUN addgroup -S docker-group && adduser -S docker-user -G docker-group

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy your orchestration service files
COPY . .

# Set permissions for the Docker socket
RUN chown docker-user:docker-group /var/run/docker.sock

# Switch to non-root user
USER docker-user

# Command to run your orchestration service
CMD ["./your-orchestration-service"] 

# Note: Ensure to run the container with the Docker socket mounted
# Example: docker run -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock --privileged your-image-name
